title: pilosella koernickeana subsp. samoviae
intro: Pilosella koernickeana subsp. samoviae, also known as the Samoyed's mouse-ear or Russian mouse-ear, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Pilosella koernickeana subsp. samoviae can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is often used in herbal medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
